paper_title: Supervised Classification for Pollen-Based Biome Prediction

methods.description: Process of using supervised classification to predict biomes from pollen data, involving model training, hyper-parameter optimization, and evaluation.

steps.description: Datasets used for supervised classification typically consists of a list of examples, each of which consists of a set of features and a target label.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare dataset with features and target labels for classification
outputs: prepared dataset with features and labels

steps.description: The procedure during which an algorithm learns parameters specific to a particular model using the training set is called model fitting.
The optimal set of hyper-parameters may be identified through cross validation by further splitting the training set into training and validation sets.
The predictive performance of a trained model is tested on the reserved test set.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate model performance on test dataset

steps.description: Results can be reported in a confusion matrix where the model predictions of biomes for the test examples are displayed against their true and known biome labels.
steps.searchable_summary: generate confusion matrix for model predictions

steps.description: From the confusion matrix, a number of evaluation methods may be calculated to measure how well a model performs on previously unseen data.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate evaluation metrics from confusion matrix

methods.description: Use of semi-parametric models, specifically Neural Networks, for complex relationships in pollen data classification.

steps.description: Neural Networks model is a generalization of the previously discussed LR with the addition of extra computational steps between the input feature and the output class labels.
steps.searchable_summary: implement neural network model for classification

steps.description: Training a NN model involves finding an optimal set of weights; this is done by minimizing the error, or loss, over the training data via gradient descent using the back-propagation algorithm.
steps.searchable_summary: train neural network using gradient descent and back-propagation

methods.description: Training of various machine learning models for biome classification using modern pollen data.

steps.description: Data were preprocessed as follows; firstly, pollen abundance data were scaled to the range 0-1.
steps.searchable_summary: scale pollen abundance data to range 0-1

steps.description: Biomes represented by less than 10 sites were removed to improve statistical requirements for sample representativeness.
steps.searchable_summary: filter out biomes with less than 10 sites

steps.description: Removed rare pollen taxa such that only taxa present above 3% in at least 1 site were kept.
steps.searchable_summary: remove rare pollen taxa below 3% presence

steps.description: The biome-labelled modern pollen dataset was divided into a training and test set in a 9:1 ratio and maintaining class distribution.
steps.searchable_summary: split dataset into training and test sets maintaining class distribution
